investment.market: No - Las Vegas wins by over 17.5 points

entry_snapshot.market_read: Connecticut hanging within volatility band despite offensive inefficiency

phase_1_game_flow.summary: Connecticut initially stayed within range, but Vegas created a second-half avalanche driven by paint dominance, transition scoring, offensive rebounding, and Sun offensive collapse.
phase_1_game_flow.turning_point: Aces extended margin through sustained rim pressure and bench scoring while Connecticut shooting cratered below recovery threshold.

phase_2_pivotal_moments: A'ja Wilson established total interior control with 22 points and 11 rebounds in only 21 minutes
Carter exploded off bench with 27 points on 13-16 FG
Connecticut shot only 32.9% FG and 23.1% from three
Sun offensive possessions repeatedly died in half-court isolation

key_failure_points: Connecticut lacked shot creation under pressure
Vegas bench efficiency exceeded median expectation
Sun defensive resistance collapsed during second-half runs
Aces maintained elite efficiency despite low three-point volume

phase_4_model_relevance_summary.model_alignment: Entry logic valid at time of strike. Collapse event and margin acceleration invalidated spread resistance thesis.
future_adjustments: Increase collapse weighting when opponent offensive efficiency drops below 35% FG
Add interior domination volatility gate for A'ja Wilson led blowout scenarios
Flag bench-efficiency eruption events when secondary scorers exceed 75% TS
